sourceMovie.title: Wish
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Wish (2023) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Frozen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wish clicked for you, Frozen (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wish clicked for you, Aladdin (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wish?
faq.answer: Try Frozen, Aladdin, Alice in Wonderland—each shares DNA with Wish while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wish?
faq.answer: Wish (2023) is best described as Animation, Family, Fantasy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wish worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
